s-1.ptxt: INSTALL BLOWER ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the blower assembly with the 2 screws.
Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL BLOWER ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the blower assembly with the 6 screws.
Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COOLER EXPANSION VALVE (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Using a 4 mm hexagon wrench, install the cooler expansion valve with the 2 hexagon bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL REINFORCEMENT ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the conditioner unit assembly to the instrument panel reinforcement assembly with the 2 screws and 3 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Tighten the bolts and screws in the order shown in the illustration to install the air conditioner unit assembly.

s2.ptxt: Driver seat:

s3.ptxt: Using a "TORX" socket wrench (T40), install the instrument panel reinforcement assembly with the 3 "TORX" bolts.

s2.ptxt: Passenger seat:

s3.ptxt: Using a 12 mm hexagon wrench, adjust the position of the 2 collars.
Using a "TORX" socket wrench (T40), install the instrument panel reinforcement assembly with the 2 "TORX" bolts.

s2.ptxt: Install the instrument panel reinforcement assembly with the 2 bolts and nut.
Install the 2 bolts and 2 caps.
Connect the connectors and clamps.
Install the 7 nuts and 6 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the air conditioning amplifier assembly with the 2 bolts.
Connect the connectors.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING COLUMN ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the steering column assembly with the bolt and 2 nuts.
Connect the connectors and wire harness clamps to the steering column assembly.
Align the matchmarks on the steering intermediate shaft assembly and the power steering link assembly.
Install the bolt.
Install the clamp to the steering column hole shield.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING POST ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the steering post assembly with the bolt and 2 nuts.
Connect the connectors and wire harness clamps to the steering post assembly.
Align the matchmarks on the steering sliding yoke and the power steering link assembly.
Install the bolt.
Install the clamp to the steering column hole shield.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 AIR DUCT

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws to install the No. 1 air duct.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR NO. 1 AIR DUCT

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws to install the rear No. 1 air duct.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR NO. 2 AIR DUCT

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws to install the rear No. 2 air duct.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FLOOR CARPET BRACKET RH
INSTALL FLOOR CARPET BRACKET LH
INSTALL NO. 1 CONSOLE BOX DUCT (for Automatic Air Conditioning System)

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 1 console box duct with the clip.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL SAFETY PAD ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 5 claws.

atten3.ptxt: Do not allow the wire harness to get caught in the claws.

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 bolts <C> or <H> and 2 nuts <I> or <Z>.
Engage each clamp.
Install the bolt <J>.
Install the 2 passenger airbag bolts <K>.
Connect each connector and install the instrument panel safety pad assembly.
with Plasmacluster:

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL SAFETY PAD ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 5 claws.

atten3.ptxt: Do not allow the wire harness to get caught in the claws.

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 bolts <N>, <a> or <b> and 2 nuts <U>.
Engage each clamp.
Install the bolt <Y>.
Install the 2 passenger airbag bolts <V>.
Connect each connector and install the instrument panel safety pad assembly.
with Plasmacluster:

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 DEFROSTER NOZZLE GARNISH

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Engage the 4 guides.
Engage the 8 clips and install the No. 1 defroster nozzle garnish.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT NO. 2 SPEAKER ASSEMBLY (for LH Side)

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Install the front No. 2 speaker assembly with the 2 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 1 SPEAKER PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 guides.
Engage the 6 claws and the 2 clips to install the instrument panel No. 1 speaker panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 1 REGISTER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 clips and install the instrument panel No. 1 register assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT PILLAR GARNISH LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the protective cover.
Install a new clip <A> on the front pillar garnish LH.
Engage the 2 claws and 2 clips, then install the front pillar garnish LH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT NO. 2 SPEAKER ASSEMBLY (for RH Side)

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 2 SPEAKER PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 guides.
Engage the 6 claws and the 2 clips to install the instrument panel No. 2 speaker panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 3 REGISTER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 clips and install the instrument panel No. 3 register assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT PILLAR GARNISH RH

s2.ptxt: Remove the protective cover.
Install a new clip <A> on the front pillar garnish RH.
Engage the 2 claws and 2 clips, then install the front pillar garnish RH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 CONSOLE BOX INSERT FRONT (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip.
Install the No. 1 console box insert front with the 3 screws <F>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 CONSOLE BOX INSERT FRONT (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip.
Install the No. 1 console box insert front with the 3 screws <O>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 2 CONSOLE BOX INSERT FRONT (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip.
Install the No. 2 console box insert front with the 3 screws <F>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 2 CONSOLE BOX INSERT FRONT (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip.
Install the No. 2 console box insert front with the 3 screws <O>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CONSOLE BOX ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 screws <F>.
Install the console box assembly with the 2 bolts <G>.
Engage the clamp.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CONSOLE BOX ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 screws <O>.
Install the console box assembly with the 2 bolts <S> or <T>.
Engage the clamp.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CONSOLE BOX CARPET

s2.ptxt: Install the console box carpet.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CONSOLE BOX POCKET

s2.ptxt: Install the console box pocket.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL RADIO RECEIVER WITH HEATER CONTROL PANEL ASSEMBLY (w/o Navigation System)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Engage the 4 clips.
Install the radio receiver with heater control panel assembly with the 4 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NAVIGATION RECEIVER WITH HEATER CONTROL PANEL ASSEMBLY (w/ Navigation System)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Engage the 4 clips.
Install the navigation receiver with heater control panel assembly with the 4 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 2 REGISTER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Engage the 7 clips and install the instrument panel No. 2 register assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UPPER CONSOLE PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Engage the 4 claws.
Install the upper console panel sub-assembly with the 2 screws <F>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UPPER CONSOLE PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Engage the 4 claws.
Install the upper console panel sub-assembly with the 2 screws <O>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UPPER CONSOLE REAR PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Engage the 3 claws and 5 clips to install the upper console rear panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UPPER CONSOLE REAR PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 3 claws and 5 clips to install the upper console rear panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FLOOR SHIFT POSITION INDICATOR HOUSING SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: with Seat Heater System:
Engage the 6 claws and the 3 clips to install the floor shift position indicator housing sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UPPER CONSOLE PANEL (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws and the 5 clips to install the upper console panel as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 2 INSTRUMENT CLUSTER FINISH PANEL GARNISH

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 clips and install the No. 2 instrument cluster finish panel garnish.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 INSTRUMENT CLUSTER FINISH PANEL GARNISH

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 clips and install the No. 1 instrument cluster finish panel garnish.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL SHIFT LEVER KNOB SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Install the shift lever knob sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL SHIFT LEVER KNOB SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Install the shift lever knob sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Engage the 3 claws and 3 clips.
Install the 4 screws <B>.
Install the lower instrument panel sub-assembly with the bolt <A>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 3 claws and 3 clips.

Install the screw or

and 3 screws .
Install the lower instrument panel sub-assembly with the bolt <N>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT PANEL NO. 2 UNDER COVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 claws and 2 guides and install the instrument panel No. 2 under cover.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL SIDE TRIM SUB-ASSEMBLY RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT DOOR SCUFF PLATE RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Install the combination meter assembly with the 4 screws <E>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Connect each connector.
Install the combination meter assembly with the 4 screws <R>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INSTRUMENT CLUSTER FINISH PANEL

s2.ptxt: Engage the guide and the 4 claws.
Install the instrument cluster finish panel with the 2 clips.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL FINISH PANEL (w/o Smart Key System)

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws and 2 clips to install the lower instrument panel finish panel.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL FINISH PANEL (w/ Smart Key System)

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Engage the 2 claws and 2 clips to install the lower instrument panel finish panel.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 INSTRUMENT PANEL SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.
Engage the 3 claws and 2 clips to install the No. 1 instrument panel sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL TURN SIGNAL SWITCH ASSEMBLY WITH SPIRAL CABLE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the turn signal switch assembly with spiral cable sub-assembly to the steering column assembly with the clamp.
Connect the connectors to the turn signal switch assembly with spiral cable sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: ADJUST SPIRAL CABLE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Check that the ignition switch is off.
Check that the battery negative (-) cable is disconnected.

atten2.ptxt: Wait for 90 seconds after disconnecting the cable to prevent airbag deployment.

s2.ptxt: Rotate the spiral cable counterclockwise slowly by hand until it feels firm.

atten3.ptxt: Do not turn the spiral cable by the airbag wire harness.

s2.ptxt: Rotate the spiral cable clockwise approximately 2.5 turns to align the marks.

atten3.ptxt: Do not turn the spiral cable by the airbag wire harness.

atten4.ptxt: The spiral cable will rotate approximately 2.5 turns to both the left and right from the center.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING COLUMN COVER (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the upper steering column cover.
Engage the 2 claws and install the lower steering column cover.
Install the steering column cover with the 2 screws <D>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING COLUMN COVER (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the upper steering column cover.
Engage the 2 claws and install the lower steering column cover.
Install the steering column cover with the 2 screws <Q>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL FINISH PANEL LH (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the air hose and connect the connector.
Engage the claw and the 9 clips.
Instal the instrument panel finish panel LH with the screw <B> and bolt <A>.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER INSTRUMENT PANEL FINISH PANEL LH (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the air hose and connect the connector.
Engage the claw and the 9 clips.

Instal the instrument panel finish panel LH with the screw or

and bolt .

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL SIDE TRIM SUB-ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 clips.
Install the cowl side trim sub-assembly LH with the cowl side trim clip.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT DOOR SCUFF PLATE LH

s2.ptxt: Engage the 7 claws and 3 clips, then install the front door scuff plate LH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING WHEEL ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Align the matchmarks on the steering wheel assembly and steering main shaft.
Install the steering wheel assembly set nut.
Connect the connectors to the spiral cable sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING PAD

s2.ptxt: With the steering pad installed on the vehicle, perform a visual check. If there are any defects as mentioned below, replace the steering pad with a new one:

item.ptxt: Cuts, minute cracks or marked discoloration on the steering pad top surface or in the grooved portion.

s2.ptxt: Make sure that the horn sounds.

atten4.ptxt: If the horn does not sound, inspect the horn system (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER NO. 3 STEERING WHEEL COVER

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 3 lower steering wheel cover.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL LOWER NO. 2 STEERING WHEEL COVER

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 2 lower steering wheel cover.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL HEATER INLET WATER HOSE

s2.ptxt: Install the water hose and attach the clip.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL HEATER OUTLET WATER HOSE

s2.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the heater inlet water hose.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL AIR CONDITIONER TUBE AND ACCESSORY

s2.ptxt: Remove the attached vinyl tape from the tube.
Sufficiently apply compressor oil to a new O-ring and fitting surface of the air conditioning tube assembly.
Install the O-ring on the air conditioner tube and accessory.
Install the air conditioner tube and accessory.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL SUCTION HOSE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the attached vinyl tape from the hose.
Sufficiently apply compressor oil to a new O-ring and the fitting surface of the suction hose sub-assembly.
Install the O-ring on the suction hose sub-assembly.
Move the hook connector in the direction indicated by the arrow in the illustration.
Insert the pipe joint into the fitting hole securely and tighten the bolt.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL WINDSHIELD WIPER MOTOR AND LINK ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the windshield wiper motor and link assembly with the 4 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Tighten the bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL TOP VENTILATOR LOUVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 claws and install the cowl top ventilator louver sub-assembly.
Install the 2 clips.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL LH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal LH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL RH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal RH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly LH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly RH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s2.ptxt: Operate the front wipers while spraying washer fluid on the windshield glass. Make sure that the front wipers function properly and the wipers do not come into contact with the vehicle body.

s-1.ptxt: CONNECT NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL
INSPECT STEERING PAD

s2.ptxt: With the steering pad installed on the vehicle, perform a visual check. If there are any defects as mentioned below, replace the steering pad with a new one:

item.ptxt: Cuts, minute cracks or marked discoloration on the steering pad top surface or in the grooved portion.

s2.ptxt: Make sure that the horn sounds.

atten4.ptxt: If the horn does not sound, inspect the horn system (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT SRS WARNING LIGHT
ADD ENGINE COOLANT (for 2AZ-FE)

s2.ptxt: Close the radiator drain cock plug and 2 cylinder block drain cock plugs.

torqueitem.ptxt: for cylinder block drain cock plug

s2.ptxt: Slowly fill the radiator with TOYOTA Super Long Life Coolant (SLLC).

item.ptxt: TOYOTA vehicles are filled with TOYOTA SLLC at the factory. In order to avoid damage to the engine cooling system and other technical problems, only use TOYOTA SLLC or similar high quality ethylene glycol based non-silicate, non-amine, non-nitrite, non-borate coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology (coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology consists of a combination of low phosphates and organic acids).
Contact your TOYOTA dealer for further details.

s2.ptxt: Slowly pour coolant into the radiator reservoir tank until it reaches the FULL line.
Press the inlet and outlet radiator hoses several times by hand, and then check the level of the coolant.
If the coolant level is low, add coolant.
Install the radiator cap sub-assembly and reservoir tank cap.
Start the engine, and warm it up.

atten4.ptxt: Adjust the air conditioner set temperature to MAX (HOT).

s2.ptxt: Stop the engine, and wait until the engine coolant cools down.
Add engine coolant to the FULL line on the radiator reservoir.

s-1.ptxt: ADD ENGINE COOLANT (for 2GR-FE)

s2.ptxt: Close the radiator drain cock plug and 2 cylinder block drain cock plugs.

torqueitem.ptxt: for cylinder block drain cock plug

s2.ptxt: Slowly fill the radiator with TOYOTA Super Long Life Coolant (SLLC).

item.ptxt: TOYOTA vehicles are filled with TOYOTA SLLC at the factory. In order to avoid damage to the engine cooling system and other technical problems, only use TOYOTA SLLC or similar high quality ethylene glycol based non-silicate, non-amine, non-nitrite, non-borate coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology (coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology consists of a combination of low phosphates and organic acids).
Contact your TOYOTA dealer for further details.

s2.ptxt: Slowly pour coolant into the radiator reservoir tank until it reaches the FULL line.
Press the inlet and outlet radiator hoses several times by hand, and then check the level of the coolant.
If the coolant level is low, add coolant.
Install the radiator cap sub-assembly and reservoir tank cap.
Start the engine, and warm it up.

atten4.ptxt: Adjust the air conditioner set temperature to MAX (HOT).

s2.ptxt: Stop the engine, and wait until the engine coolant cools down.
Add engine coolant to the FULL line on the radiator reservoir.

s-1.ptxt: CHECK FOR ENGINE COOLANT LEAKS
CHARGE WITH REFRIGERANT

s2.ptxt: Perform vacuum purging using a vacuum pump.
Charge with refrigerant HFC-134a (R134a).

item.ptxt: Do not turn the A/C on before charging with refrigerant. Doing so will cause the cooler compressor to work without refrigerant, resulting in overheating of the cooler compressor.
Approximately 100 g (3.53 oz.) of refrigerant may need to be charged after bubbles disappear.
The refrigerant amount should be checked by quantity, not with the sight glass.

atten4.ptxt: Ensure that sufficient refrigerant is available to recharge the system when using a refrigerant recovery unit. Refrigerant recovery units are not always able to recover 100 % of the refrigerant from an A/C system.

s-1.ptxt: WARM UP ENGINE

s2.ptxt: Keep the A/C switch on for at least 2 minutes to warm up the compressor.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to warm up the compressor when turning the A/C on after removing and installing the cooler refrigerant lines (including the compressor), to prevent damage to the compressor.

s-1.ptxt: CHECK FOR REFRIGERANT LEAKS

s2.ptxt: After recharging with refrigerant gas, check for leakage of refrigerant gas using a halogen leak detector.
Carry out the test under the following conditions:

item.ptxt: Secure good ventilation (the gas leak detector may react to volatile gases which are not refrigerant, such as evaporated gasoline and exhaust gas).
Make sure that there is some refrigerant remaining in the refrigeration system.
When the compressor is off: approx. 392 to 588 kPa (4 to 6 kgf/cm2, 57 to 85 psi)

s2.ptxt: Using a gas leak detector, check for leakage from the refrigerant lines.
Bring the gas leak detector close to the drain hose with the detector's power off, and then turn the detector on.

item.ptxt: After the blower motor has stopped, let the cooling unit stand for more than 15 minutes.
Bring the gas leak detector sensor under the drain hose.
When bringing the gas leak detector close to the drain hose, make sure that the gas leak detector does not react to volatile gases.
If it is not possible to avoid interference from volatile gases, the vehicle should be lifted up to allow testing.

s2.ptxt: If a gas leak is not detected from the drain hose, remove the blower motor control from the cooling unit. Insert the gas leak detector sensor into the unit and perform the test.
Disconnect the pressure switch connector and leave it for approximately 20 minutes. Bring the gas leak detector close to the pressure switch and perform the test.
